title: Clippy Feature
main_idea: I want to be able to, within the app, design characters that can then be assigned to a session, and would tap into the ai through injection and be the 'voice' of the agent.
reason: I want to add this gimmick because it would be fun.  It should remain completely optional.
requirements: we would want some sort of studio where you can design these characters.  DiceBear has a sweet package we can leverage, but im open to even better options.

requirements.description: characters would not just have a look, but you would give them a persona.
requirements.ex: You're an old sailor, and you're incredibly salty that you're stuck coding, because you'd rather be fishing.  But you always do the best job, becuase that's how you were raised'

requirements: the character would be injected into the cli somehow. this we need to figure out

we monitor the terminal output, and tell the ai to wrap certain phrases in <speak></speak> tags or something similair.  we would want this to be modular enough that eventually it could support more tags.  like emotion tags to animate the character, etc
we want this character to sit on the screen in a way that is best described by referencing the microsoft word guy clippy.

requirements.description: the ai would periodically interject short quips, that either lightly comment on what the agent is doing, or puts fourth questions, or calls attention to the terminal. These would all be written in character
requirements.ex1: Looks like we're starting to plan here.
requirements.ex2: Oh that looks like a good idea.

requirements: think of this as a fun gimick.  it should not in anyway interfere with the actual user experience.
i want the character to 'say' it through a speech buubble just like clippy

types: talking (mouth from default to O shape)
thinking (shown when AI is typing, so when output is being activtely received i guess)

requirements: This only applies to AI terminal / clippy only reacts to that
we should NOT call this clippy, but come up with a good name to use throughout the app, and start using it in the plan right away and strip clippy from our nomenclature
we should have several defaults to start.  but let's make a single for now to test.
we should be able to select a clippy through keyboard shortcuts as well as UI
we should have a studio that allows creating and modifying these clippys, both configuration wise AND visually.
studio should be accessible and navigable by keyboard shortcuts
ui_notes: we want to make sure we show the clippy somewhere that won't be too intrusive.  we could dedicate a chunk of the tools panel specifically to it, agnostic of the tool tab we have selected. it should default to no avatar, and when there is none selected, ui to select it
open_questions: How is the persona injected into the cli?
Can we monitor the terminal and make the clippy respond to tags?
